D11 ComputationalL3 ApplyReference

Floyd–Steinberg Dither — Error Diffusion

Round each pixel to the nearest level, then spread the quantisation error to neighbours in a 4-cell pattern (7/16, 3/16, 5/16, 1/16) — the default dither in 1976 PDP-11 graphics

Floyd-Steinberg averages the quantisation error spatially, so smooth gradients survive even with a 1-bit palette. The "wormy" texture is characteristic — high-frequency noise that approximates the original intensity locally. Used by every GIF encoder, the GameBoy Camera, and Apple Mac System 1.

Slide levels (2 = pure black/white, 8 = subtle).
